englishDefinitions: This is not a common standard phrasal verb in English.
In some contexts, it may be a mistaken form of 'laugh at' or 'laugh someone out of something' (to make someone leave by laughing).
persianTranslation: به خاطر خندیدن از جایی بیرون کردن
examples: Laugh out of is not usually used as a fixed phrase.
You may hear 'laugh someone out of the room' instead.
The speaker likely meant a different expression, not laugh out of.
